page: Amazon Web Services m8a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test and Geekbench single-core score both ranking in the top 10%. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier throughput. However, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with Passmark memory scores, consistently register average performance. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong image processing and small-model LLM prompt processing, though text generation for medium-sized models remains average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8a.2xlarge offers high-frequency single-core execution and modern DDR5 memory speeds within a standard general-purpose resource density. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage-heavy or parallelized graphics workloads must rely on external network-attached resources. This instance is well-suited for applications requiring strong single-threaded CPU performance, such as web serving, text processing, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, offering 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by high stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression speeds. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with average tier performance. With a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps, the m8a.2xlarge represents a balanced resource profile. It is best suited for workloads prioritizing single-threaded CPU speed, such as web serving, text processing, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining high-frequency AMD EPYC single-core performance with fast DDR5 memory for efficient compute workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached resources
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and light LLM inference
